completed_tasks.name: Round 1: root-cause + fix buffer-growth no-op on live hlsdemux2
Round 1: real-world verify — buffer now reaches 100%
Round 2: root-cause residual segment-fetch stalls + ship instrumentation (cause_hint=segment_retry, hls_segment_fetch_issue log)
Commit both rounds as separate atomic commits on fix branch

remaining_tasks.name: USER: run fixed build on a lofi live station; on next rebuffer inspect ~/.local/share/musicstreamer/buffer-events.log for cause_hint=segment_retry + hls_segment_fetch_issue lines

Round 3 (gated): if segment_retry confirmed, build stall-duration watchdog / forced re-seek to cap 100+s stalls. If still cause_hint=unknown with no hls_segment_fetch_issue, reopen (silent sub-Bin stall).
Decide push/PR for branch fix/yt-live-drops-buffer-underrun (not pushed, no PR)

blockers.description: Round 3 fix is gated on real-world log evidence that segment_retry is the true driver — cannot proceed without a user listening session
blockers.workaround: Round 2 instrumentation already shipped to gather that evidence

human_actions_pending.action: Run the fixed build during a real multi-minute YouTube-live stall and inspect buffer-events.log
human_actions_pending.context: Only a real session validates the Round 2 instrumentation and unlocks the evidence-based Round 3 watchdog decision

decisions.decision: Split the intermingled player.py diff into two atomic commits (R1 buffer-growth, R2 instrumentation)
decisions.rationale: User asked for separate atomic commits; done via full-file backup -> reduce to R1-only -> commit -> restore -> commit R2

decisions.decision: Defer the stall-duration watchdog to Round 3 rather than build it now
decisions.rationale: Insufficient evidence yet that a forced re-seek helps; ship instrumentation first to confirm cause=segment_retry (user chose 'wait for evidence')

decisions.decision: Residual 108-172s full drains are primarily env/YouTube-CDN-side, not player-eliminable at the source
decisions.rationale: No buffer covers a multi-minute upstream segment gap; player's only lever is faster forced recovery

next_action: Resume ONLY after the user reports a real fixed-build session: read ~/.local/share/musicstreamer/buffer-events.log for cause_hint=segment_retry / hls_segment_fetch_issue around a buffer_underrun, then either build the Round 3 watchdog or `/gsd:debug continue yt-live-drops-buffer-underrun` if evidence points elsewhere.
context_notes: Two-root-cause investigation. R1 (buffer growth never propagated to running hlsdemux2 element) is FIXED and verified — buffer now hits 100%. R2 shipped observability for the residual segment-supply stalls, which are largely YouTube-side. Everything is committed on branch fix/yt-live-drops-buffer-underrun (2 commits, 60 tests total). Debug session file status is awaiting_human_verify. See memory yt-live-drops-two-root-causes.md.
